latest.title: Zegona promoted into the MSCI United Kingdom Index
latest.content:
Today 15:34
RNS Number : 7797U
Zegona Communications PLC
27 February 2026
27 February 2026
Zegona Communications plc
Zegona promoted into the MSCI United Kingdom Index
Zegona Communications plc (the "
Company
") announces that its ordinary shares are promoted into the MSCI United Kingdom Index as of the close of today, 27 February 2026. The MSCI United Kingdom Index measures the performance of the large and mid cap segments of the UK market and is tracked by a number of Exchange Traded Funds (ETFs) and active managers.
For further information please contact:
Investor enquiries:
Media enquiries:
Alfonso Enríquez
Jaime De Andres
Tilly Abraham (Sodali & Co)
info
@zegona.com
jaime.andres@vodafone.com
zegona@info.sodali.com
About Zegona
Zegona is publicly listed on the Main Market of the LSE. It was established in 2015 with the objective of investing in businesses in the European Telecommunications, Media and Technology sector and improving their performance to deliver attractive shareholder returns. Zegona is led by former Virgin Media executives Eamonn O'Hare and Robert Samuelson. In 2024, Zegona completed the acquisition of Vodafone Spain.

latest.title: Zegona Holdco Limited TLB reporting as at 31/12/25
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 4295U
Zegona Communications PLC
26 February 2026
Zegona Holdco Limited TLB reporting as at 31/12/25
26 February 2026
LEI: 213800ASI1VZL2ED4S65
Zegona Communications plc
Publication of Zegona Holdco Limited's debt reporting as at 31 December 2025
·
Zegona is pleased to publish information in relation to Zegona Holdco Limited and its subsidiaries ("Zegona Holdco Group"), for the purpose of satisfying the Zegona Holdco Group's external debt reporting obligations.
·
This includes key performance indicators (KPIs) of Vodafone Spain for the three months ended 31 December 2025 as well as operational commentary summarising the strong Q3 results, which pave the way for a second successful year in Zegona's transformation of Vodafone Spain.
·
The information is set out in the appendix to this announcement.
·
The information can also be found on Zegona's website at
https://www.zegona.com/investor-relations/debt-investors.aspx
For further information, please contact:
For investor enquiries:
For media enquiries:
Alfonso Enríquez
Jaime De Andres
Tilly Abraham (Sodali & Co)
info
@zegona.com
jaime.andres@vodafone.com
zegona@info.sodali.com
About Zegona
Zegona is publicly listed on the Main Market of the LSE. It was established in 2015 with the objective of investing in businesses in the European Telecommunications, Media and Technology sector and improving their performance to deliver attractive shareholder returns.
Zegona is led by former Virgin Media executives Eamonn O'Hare and Robert Samuelson. In 2024, Zegona completed the acquisition of Vodafone Spain.
Appendix
Zegona Holdco Limited ("the Company")
A company incorporated in England and Wales, company number 10159604 with registered office address of
8 Sackville Street, London, England, W1S 3DG
Consolidated Financial Information
Unaudited
31 Dec 25
Zegona HoldCo Limited and its subsidiaries ("the Zegona HoldCo Group" or "the Group")
presents the Group's unaudited consolidated financial information for the periods noted
in the following pages.
Purpose of this information
This information is provided for the sole purpose of satisfying the Company's external debt reporting obligations as required pursuant to the Senior Facilities Agreement dated 15 July 2024.
Operational Commentary
Q3 was another strong quarter and the results pave the way for a second successful year in Zegona's transformation of Vodafone Spain. Another quarter of positive customer and product additions, together with the positive impact of the January price initiatives, gives increased confidence the company will close FY26 with revenue stabilisation, in line with prior year.
Continued implementation of business transformation initiatives is also driving profitability and cash flow. Q3 year-to-date cash flow5 of €586m means the business is well placed to close FY26 with cash flow margins over 21%. This would represent a significant improvement from last year's 17% margin and over double the 10% prior to Zegona's acquisition in FY24.
The closing of both FiberCo transactions marks another major milestone in Zegona's delivery of its post-acquisition strategy. The recent EU clearance of the FiberPass investment removes the last hurdle to completing the transformation of Vodafone Spain's fixed network strategy. The combination of FiberPass and PremiumFiber will give guaranteed access to a future-proof, all fibre national network with attractive economic terms.
The combined €1.8b upfront proceeds from the FiberCo transactions create the opportunity to deliver significant returns to our shareholders. The recently announced capital allocation policy involves returning €1.6b of value to shareholders. This included a €1.4b special dividend paid in January 2026 (permitting the settlement in full of the Vodafone Group financing and the 69% reduction in Zegona's ordinary shares) and an ongoing €0.2b share buyback programme. The remaining €0.2b will be used for debt reduction. This puts the business on track to deliver net debt of only €3.2b by the end of our financial year. This, combined with our recent debt refinancing at a cost of debt of only 4.3%, reinforces Zegona's commitment to its leverage target of 1.5x to 2x and further accelerates progress in reducing total annual interest costs.

Notes:
1
Finetwork numbers are excluded in the table above as the acquisition process is still ongoing.
2
Includes both contract and prepay mobile lines. Total Mobile contract lines have increased to 10,155k in the three months to December 2025 from 10,150k as at September 2025.
3
EBITDAaL is defined as earnings attributable to the operating group of companies before income tax credit, net financing costs, amortization of customer-related intangible assets, amortization of owned assets and depreciation of owned assets, excluding gains/losses on disposal of owned and leased assets, restructuring costs, other income and expense and significant items that are not considered by management to be reflective of the underlying performance, including the impacts of depreciation and gain on disposal of leased assets and interest on lease liabilities, and adjusted in line with the parent's accounting policy relating to subscriber acquisition costs.
4
Net debt is shown as calculated for the investor presentations, being the nominal amount of debt (with the SSN USD amounts presented as the hedged EUR value of the nominal amount) and net of cash held by the Zegona Holdco Group as at 31 December 2025 (€647m). Zegona Holdco Group EBITDAaL was €312m for the 3 months to December 2025.
5
Cash flows are defined as EBITDAaL less capital expenditure relating to acquisition of property, plant and equipment, intangible assets and costs relating to customer commercial activity, as well as payments made for dismantling assets and excluding telecommunication licences and financed assets, minus the selling price upon disposal of any assets and changes in asset retirement obligation provisions, as defined in the FY25 Annual Report.

company_30s.what_they_do: Zegona is a UK‑based telecom infrastructure firm that builds and runs fibre networks across Spain and other parts of Europe, supplying high‑capacity backhaul to mobile operators and large enterprises.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The company sits on a growing market for 5G and cloud connectivity. Its fibre assets are strategically located in key urban and rural corridors, giving it a competitive edge over smaller providers and making it an attractive partner for telecom operators seeking to expand coverage quickly.
company_30s.current_state: Zegona has recently restructured its debt, successfully reduced its borrowing costs, and begun a share‑buyback programme that has cut the share count by nearly 70%. The firm was just added to the MSCI UK Index, signalling a boost in passive exposure. Despite these positive moves, the market remains largely silent, and the stock trades at a very low multiple of its asset base.

rns_narrative: Financial optimisation: The January debt repricing lowered interest expenses, while February’s own‑share transaction and share‑cancellation move tightened the equity base.
Market positioning: Inclusion in the MSCI UK Index and clearance of the EU‑approved FiberPass transaction are likely to bring fresh institutional demand.
Dividend strategy: Regular dividend payments and an ex‑dividend date on 9 Jan 2026 have attracted income‑focused investors, creating a potential buying wave.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet optimism around dividend and index inclusion
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bulls are pointing to the dividend payout and MSCI listing as catalysts that could spark a rebound, especially for income‑seeking funds.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears worry that the share cancellation may not offset lingering liquidity concerns and that the debt restructuring could still leave the firm vulnerable to market shocks.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The chatter has stayed muted overall, but a small uptick in bullish posts appeared after the dividend announcement, suggesting a latent upside that has not yet been priced in.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat, indicating no recent search interest – a classic sign that the market has not yet priced in the story.

near_term_catalysts: The ex‑dividend date on 9 Jan 2026 – income investors may pile in ahead of the payout.
The EU clearance of the FiberPass transaction – this could unlock new contracts and improve cash flow.
MSCI UK Index inclusion – passive funds may start buying once the inclusion is processed.
Any announcement of new fibre‑deployment contracts or partnership deals – these would validate the company’s growth narrative.
A successful share‑buyback completion – this would support the price by reducing supply.
expect_next.watch_for: Key signals include a volume spike of at least twice the 30‑day average around the ex‑dividend date, a clean break above the 52‑week low, and any new contract announcements that confirm the company’s expansion plans.
expect_next.timing_regime: The APEX score shows tight compression, suggesting the stock is in the early stages of a mean‑reversion cycle. If the catalysts materialise, a rebound is likely to start within the next 1–3 months.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The deep crash has produced a 40‑plus percent average return historically for GREEN signals, and the current upside to the mid‑point between the low and pre‑crash high is roughly 200‑300%. The dividend and MSCI inclusion add liquidity and institutional interest, further supporting a rebound.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: If the debt restructuring fails to improve cash flow or if the company cannot secure new fibre contracts, the price could stay trapped at the current low. The share cancellation may not be enough to offset potential liquidity strain, leaving a realistic downside that could keep the stock flat for an extended period.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential loss is capped at the current price; upside could be 200‑300% if the mean‑reversion plays out, giving a risk‑reward ratio of about 1:3.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a small dip after the 9 Jan ex‑dividend date, allocate 10% of your contrarian capital, and set a stop 10% below the 52‑week low. Scale in as volume confirms the move.
playbook.if_watching: Hold off until you see a volume surge of at least 2× the 30‑day average and a clean break above the 52‑week low. Also watch for any new contract or partnership announcement.
playbook.if_passing: No terminal risk identified; the setup remains bullish unless a bankruptcy filing or delisting notice appears.

ai_insights.bottom_line: A deep crash combined with dividend and MSCI inclusion creates a high‑risk, high‑reward opportunity with a 200‑300% upside if mean reversion kicks in.
